title: CS 340: Distributed Systems
body: Fall 2026 · Department of Computer Science
Instructor: Prof. Elena Vasquez
Meets Tue/Thu 2:00-3:30pm, Room 220

title: Course Overview
body: Study the theory and practice of building systems that run across many machines
Topics: consensus, replication, partitioning, consistency models, fault tolerance
Format: 2 lectures/week + weekly lab + 1 group project
Prerequisites: CS 240 (Operating Systems), comfort with Go or Rust

title: Learning Objectives
body: Explain CAP theorem tradeoffs and apply them to real system designs
Implement a working Raft consensus module from scratch
Diagnose split-brain and network-partition failure modes
Design a sharded, replicated key-value store under failure
Read and critique 3 seminal distributed-systems papers

title: Weekly Topics
body: Week 1-2: Networking fundamentals + RPC
Week 3-4: Replication and consistency models
Week 5-6: Consensus — Paxos and Raft
Week 7-8: Partitioning and sharding
Week 9-10: Fault tolerance and failure detection
Week 11-12: Distributed transactions
Week 13-14: Case studies — Spanner, DynamoDB, Kafka
Week 15: Final project presentations

title: Grading Breakdown
body: Weekly labs: 25% of final grade
Midterm exam: 20% of final grade
Group project: 35% of final grade
Final exam: 15% of final grade
Participation: 5% of final grade

title: Required Reading List
body: "Designing Data-Intensive Applications" — Martin Kleppmann (primary textbook)
"In Search of an Understandable Consensus Algorithm" — Ongaro & Ousterhout (Raft paper)
"The Google File System" — Ghemawat, Gobioff, Leung
"Dynamo: Amazon's Highly Available Key-value Store" — DeCandia et al.
Weekly paper summaries posted on course site before each Thursday lecture

title: Office Hours & Support
body: Prof. Vasquez: Mon 10am-12pm and Wed 3-4pm, CS Building 412
TA Jordan Kim: Tue/Thu 4-5:30pm, CS Building lab 105
TA Priya Nair: Fri 1-3pm, CS Building lab 105
"Office hours saved me during the Raft implementation — the TAs actually debug with you line by line." — student course evaluation, Spring 2026
Async help: course Discord, response target under 24 hours
